summary: Tested Landing Page, Operational Hours APIs, Manual Toggle, Booking Availability and Salon Dashboard UI. Backend: 8/10 tests pass. Frontend: landing page, routing, and dashboard badges all working. Found 1 critical backend bug in manual-toggle endpoint.

critical.issue: Endpoint hardcodes is_overridden=True regardless of request body. Clients cannot clear the override. Since GET /is-accepting-bookings short-circuits on is_overridden, once a salon is manually toggled, the holiday-blocking code path is unreachable via API. Fix: read toggle_data.get('is_overridden') and honor it; if False, reset overridden_at to None. Also add an auto-clear-on-day-transition mechanism as per requirements.

issues: Logo text 'SalonHub' appears duplicated next to logo icon — looks like '{logo} SalonHub SalonHub'. Remove one occurrence.

action_items: Fix PUT /api/salons/{id}/manual-toggle to honor is_overridden field from request body so override can be cleared
Implement auto-clear of manual_toggle override on day transition (per requirement 'Manual toggle should remain overridden until next automatic day transition')
Remove duplicate 'SalonHub' text in landing page header
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:1615 hardcodes is_overridden=True in manual-toggle handler — ignores client intent
is-accepting-bookings unconditionally short-circuits when is_overridden=True — combined with the above bug, holiday logic becomes unreachable after any manual toggle
No scheduled job or on-read logic found to auto-clear override on day transition
context_for_next_testing_agent: Landing page at / renders with Google colors and Login button; /user/login correctly redirects to /login. Salon dashboard shows 'MANUALLY OPEN' badge in the header when override is set. Settings tab contains Operational Hours module with Monday-Sunday, Lunch and Holiday controls. Only unresolved issue: manual-toggle API cannot clear override (hardcoded True). Backend PUT operational-hours accepts body as flat OperationalHours object (NOT wrapped in {'operational_hours': ...}).
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause of manual-toggle bug: in /app/backend/server.py lines 1613-1619, update_manual_toggle() builds the stored dict with a literal 'is_overridden': True instead of reading the value from toggle_data. Reproduction: PUT /api/salons/{id}/manual-toggle with {is_overridden: false, is_open: true} and then GET /operational-hours — the stored is_overridden is still true. Mitigation: change line 1616 to 'is_overridden': toggle_data.get('is_overridden', True) and set overridden_at to None when False. Additionally implement a day-transition auto-clear (either on read comparing overridden_at date to today, or a scheduled task).
